reachability_artifacts: go test ./cmd/sworn -run V015Conformance -count=1 builds sworn and drives public CLI and MCP subprocesses through isolated temporary Git repositories, bare remotes, empty-scope PASS, overlay/Coach failures, cycle-1 boundaries, local invalidation, full rollback envelopes, reset/clean restarts, migration, ship, and exact local-only Baton mark-shipped
release_benefit: The release has one public, restart-spanning proof that Sworn enforces the exact Baton v0.15 lifecycle rather than merely carrying its schemas.

design_decisions.choice: Close the release with one test-only public conformance slice after the five-track implementation graph converges.
options: final public conformance slice over all contracts
monolithic out-of-band implementation and retrospective test
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-15 and the 2026-07-16 orchestrator recommendation: use 18 proof-bounded slices in five tracks with S18 as the final real-binary conformance boundary.
design_decisions.rationale: A final public-entry proof is needed to detect adapter and restart gaps without weakening the independently owned product contracts.

positive_scenarios: A complete real-binary lifecycle reaches local shipped bookkeeping with exact pin, empty-scope fast path, overlay, Coach, merge, re-plan, Baton ship/status/index identities plus byte-identical pure-plan board, human push/cleanup handoff, and already-shipped no-op.
negative_scenarios: Every overlay/Coach tamper, stale identity, boundary expansion, repeated failure, wrong-target restart cleanup, invalid rollback, partial standalone command, migration mismatch, or undeployed ship attempt fails closed without unauthorized mutation, push, cleanup, or redispatch.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: End-to-end public proof catches adapter and recovery gaps that isolated package tests cannot observe.
